Name: Hydrogen
Appearance: Colourless, odourless gaseous chemical element
ReactivityNote: flammable; forms water with oxygen, reactive in atomic form
Notes: colorless in pure form (white hex is a placeholder for colorless)

Name: Helium
Appearance: Colourless, odourless gaseous nonmetallic element
ReactivityNote: completely inert; forms no stable compounds
Notes: does not solidify at 1 atm at any temperature (requires elevated pressure); colorless in pure form (white hex is a placeholder for colorless); unavailable/undefined values (null): meltingPoint, electronegativity

Name: Lithium
Appearance: Socket silvery metal
ReactivityNote: highly reactive; reacts vigorously with water and air, stored under oil

Name: Beryllium
Appearance: Grey metallic element of group 2 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: reactive; reacts with water (slowly) and acids, burns in air

Name: Boron
Appearance: An element of group 13 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: intermediate reactivity; semiconducting, amphoteric tendencies

Name: Carbon
Appearance: Carbon is a member of group 14 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: moderately reactive; basis of organic chemistry, burns to CO2
Notes: multiple allotropes (graphite/diamond/amorphous); listed bulk values are for the standard/most-stable allotrope; graphite has no melting point at 1 atm; it sublimes near ~3915 K; unavailable/undefined values (null): meltingPoint

Name: Nitrogen
Appearance: Colourless, gaseous element which belongs to group 15 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: relatively inert as N2 due to strong triple bond
Notes: colorless in pure form (white hex is a placeholder for colorless)

Name: Oxygen
Appearance: A colourless, odourless gaseous element belonging to group 16 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: strong oxidizer; supports combustion, forms oxides with most elements

Name: Fluorine
Appearance: A poisonous pale yellow gaseous element belonging to group 17 of the periodic table (The halogens)
ReactivityNote: most reactive element known; oxidizes nearly all materials

Name: Neon
Appearance: Colourless gaseous element of group 18 on the periodic table (noble gases)
ReactivityNote: completely inert; forms no stable compounds
Notes: unavailable/undefined values (null): electronegativity

Name: Sodium
Appearance: Soft silvery reactive element belonging to group 1 of the periodic table (alkali metals)
ReactivityNote: very reactive; reacts vigorously with water releasing hydrogen

Name: Magnesium
Appearance: Silvery metallic element belonging to group 2 of the periodic table (alkaline-earth metals)
ReactivityNote: reactive; reacts with water (slowly) and acids, burns in air

Name: Aluminum
Appearance: Silvery-white lustrous metallic element of group 3 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: reactive but rapidly passivates with a protective oxide layer

Name: Silicon
Appearance: Metalloid element belonging to group 14 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: intermediate reactivity; semiconducting, amphoteric tendencies

Name: Phosphorus
Appearance: Non-metallic element belonging to group 15 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: white allotrope ignites spontaneously in air; red allotrope more stable
Notes: values are for white phosphorus (the conventional reference state); red phosphorus is more thermodynamically stable (mp ~852 K)

Name: Sulfur
Appearance: Yellow, nonmetallic element belonging to group 16 of the periodic table
ReactivityNote: moderately reactive; burns to SO2, forms many compounds
Notes: melting point is the conventional ~388 K value (monoclinic); rhombic alpha-sulfur is most stable at STP and converts to monoclinic at ~369 K before melting

Name: Chlorine
Appearance: Halogen element
ReactivityNote: highly reactive oxidizer; toxic, attacks most metals and tissue

Name: Argon
Appearance: Monatomic noble gas
